items.question: Who uses Plotly in a typical ML Python visualization stack?
items.answer: Data scientists and ML engineers use Plotly for interactive notebook EDA, HTML experiment sharing, and Dash monitoring apps—alongside Matplotlib and Seaborn for static figures, not as a wholesale replacement.

items.question: What is the difference between Plotly Express and Graph Objects?
items.answer: Plotly Express (px) is a high-level DataFrame API that picks trace types from column mappings; Graph Objects (go) builds figures trace-by-trace with explicit go.Scatter, go.Bar, and layout control. Express returns a GO Figure you can still customize.

items.question: When should you choose Plotly over Matplotlib for an ML task?
items.answer: Choose Plotly when stakeholders need zoom, hover metadata, legend toggling, or a shareable HTML file—common for training-curve inspection, error-analysis scatters, and embedding walkthroughs. Choose Matplotlib when the deliverable is a fixed PNG/PDF for a paper or slide.

items.question: Where does Plotly sit relative to Seaborn and embedding visualization?
items.answer: Plotly owns interactive rendering and hover affordances; Seaborn owns statistical plotting defaults on Matplotlib; embedding visualization owns how to read and present UMAP/t-SNE/PCA coordinate plots—not the low-level trace syntax taught here.

items.question: Why configure hover_data or hovertemplate on ML diagnostic plots?
items.answer: Coordinates alone rarely identify mislabeled rows or misclassified points; hover fields (sample id, y_true, y_pred, split) turn interactivity into actionable error analysis without annotating every marker on the canvas.

items.question: How do you share an interactive training-metric figure outside Jupyter?
items.answer: Build the figure with Express or GO, tune layout and hovermode, then call fig.write_html('metrics.html', include_plotlyjs='cdn'). Recipients open the file in a browser with zoom and hover intact—no notebook server required.

items.question: Plotly Express vs Graph Objects: when to use each in ML workflows?
items.answer: Use Express for tidy DataFrame EDA—px.scatter, px.histogram, px.line with color and hover_data. Use Graph Objects when you need mixed trace types on one axes, custom hovertemplate with customdata, dual y-axes, or make_subplots with per-row trace placement Express cannot express in one call.

items.question: Plotly write_html vs Matplotlib savefig for ML deliverables?
items.answer: write_html preserves interactivity (hover, zoom, legend toggle) for internal review and stakeholder exploration. savefig produces static raster/vector output suited to publications and slides where interaction is unnecessary and file weight must stay small.

items.question: Cause: training scatter built with 500k SVG markers and no downsampling. Effect?
items.answer: The browser tab becomes sluggish or unresponsive, hover latency spikes, and reviewers cannot explore the plot—undermining the main reason Plotly was chosen over a static Matplotlib snapshot.

items.question: Cause: make_subplots without shared_xaxes on epoch-aligned loss and accuracy panels. Effect?
items.answer: Hover and zoom no longer stay synchronized across metrics; comparing val loss divergence at the same epoch as a sudden F1 drop requires manual eyeballing across misaligned x-axes.
